body_txt: Normally, when a villain who's a Bad Boss kills off a minion, it is for You Have Failed Me or You Have Outlived Your Usefulness reasons, but not always. Sometimes, the minion in question is too good at their job, and their superior will off them themselves (or congratulate the heroes if they do). The reasons for doing this vary, but are generally some combination of fear of the minion becoming The Starscream , fear that a particularly unstable minion will ruin the boss' schemes , and Even Evil Has Standards . The Dragon-in-Chief is often a target of this (although by their very nature they may survive such attempts to get rid of them by an inferior villain), as are the Underling with an F in PR and the Overzealous Underling . Very common in Real Life dictatorships since holding on to power in such an environment automatically breeds paranoia , leading to the ruler's inner circle gradually getting depleted until only the Yes-Men are left (unless one of the minions manages to bump the ruler off first — at which point the cycle begins again ). Super-Trope to Preemptive Threat Elimination . Uriah Gambit and Please Shoot the Messenger are subtropes. Tall Poppy Syndrome is often the supertrope.
